name: tifffile (TIFF File I/O for Scientific Imaging)

performance.notes: Chunked reading for large TIFFs; memory-mapped access; zarr backend for lazy loading

agent_readiness.notes: Scientific TIFF file I/O for Python. import tifffile as tif. image = tif.imread('scan.tif'). tif.imwrite('out.tif', array, photometric='rgb'). Multi-page: tif.TiffFile('stack.tif').pages[0].asarray(). Zarr: store = tif.imread('big.tif', aszarr=True). OME-TIFF: tif.imwrite('ome.tif', data, photometric='rgb', description=ome_xml). BigTIFF: >4GB files. Compression: LZW, JPEG, Zstd, Deflate, LZMA. SVS (aperio): tif.TiffFile('slide.svs'). Used in microscopy (OME-TIFF), whole-slide imaging, astronomy.

gotchas: Actively developed but changelog and API can change without notice
Documentation is thin — README and docstrings are primary references
OME-TIFF metadata parsing requires understanding OME-XML schema
Large multi-page TIFFs should use zarr backend to avoid loading into memory
best_when: Whole slide images (SVS, NDPI format via TIFF reader)
When BigTIFF (>4GB) or compressed TIFF is required
avoid_when: Standard photo TIFF (Pillow handles most cases)
When Pillow's simpler TIFF support covers the use case
summary: tifffile provides scientific TIFF file I/O for Python. tifffile.imread('scan.tif') returns numpy array. imwrite('out.tif', data). Multi-page stacks, OME-TIFF, BigTIFF (>4GB). Zarr backend for lazy loading: imread('big.tif', aszarr=True). LZW/JPEG/Zstd compression. BSD-3. Used in microscopy, whole-slide imaging, astronomy. For standard photos Pillow is simpler; tifffile excels at scientific/multi-channel/OME-TIFF.
